        i) =

              ⇑(@Matrix.sharedEigenbasis d inst✝² inst✝¹ 𝕜 inst✝ A B hA hB hAB) *

        i i)

    intro i
    open Matrix in
    open Matrix.IsHermitian in
    simp only [OrthonormalBasis.coe_toBasis,
      EuclideanSpace.basisFun_apply, toEuclideanLin_apply, EuclideanSpace.ofLp_single,
      mulVec_single, MulOpposite.op_one, one_smul, col_diagonal, diag_apply,
      EuclideanSpace.toLp_single]
    rw [Matrix.col_mulVec, Matrix.mul_mulVec, Matrix.mul_mulVec]
    let V := Matrix.sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB
    let E := (EuclideanSpace.basisFun d 𝕜).toBasis
    -- U.val is E.toMatrix V
    have hU : (Matrix.sharedEigenvectorUnitary hA hB hAB).val = E.toMatrix V.toBasis := rfl
    -- U* is V.toMatrix E
    have hU_inv : (Matrix.sharedEigenvectorUnitary hA hB hAB).valᴴ = V.toBasis.toMatrix E := by
      rw [hU, ← Matrix.inv_eq_conjTranspose_of_isUnit (Basis.toMatrix_orthonormalBasis_mem_unitary E V).isUnit, Basis.toMatrix_inv]
    rw [hU_inv, hU]
    -- Simplify the matrix-vector products
    have h1 : V.toBasis.toMatrix E *ᵥ (Pi.single i 1) = V i := by
      rw [Matrix.mulVec_single, Basis.toMatrix_col, Basis.repr_eq_self]
    rw [h1]
    -- For B, we assume sharedEigenbasis diagonalizes B as well.
    -- We need mulVec_sharedEigenbasis for B.
    -- Since sharedEigenbasis is formed from common eigenspaces, its vectors are eigenvectors of B too.
    -- However, we don't have a direct lemma `mulVec_sharedEigenbasis_B`.
    -- But `sharedEigenbasis` vectors are in `eigenspace A ... ⊓ eigenspace B ...`.
    -- So they are eigenvectors of B.
    have h_eig_B : B *ᵥ (V i) = (RCLike.re (dotProduct (V i) (B *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (V i)))) • (V i) := by
      let b := V.toBasis
      have : V i ∈ Submodule.span 𝕜 (Set.range V) := by simp [b]
      -- This path is long. Instead we use the construction of sharedEigenbasis.
      -- It is a direct sum of intersections of eigenspaces.
      -- Every basis vector comes from some eigenspace intersection.
      -- So it is an eigenvector of B.
      -- Let's extract the eigenvalue from the construction implies eigenvector property.
      -- Or use the fact that if A, B commute, and we constructed it this way...
      -- Actually, we can assume there is a `sharedEigenvalueB` analogous to A, but it wasn't defined in the snippet.
      -- We can define the eigenvalue locally as `dotProduct (V i) (B *ᵥ V i)`.
      -- And prove it is an eigenvector.
      -- The basis `V` is `LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis`.
      -- `LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis` comes from `subordinateOrthonormalBasis`.
      -- The indexing set is `Eigenvalues A × Eigenvalues B`.
      -- `V i` corresponds to some `(μ_A, μ_B)`.
      -- So `V i` is in `eigenspace A μ_A ⊓ eigenspace B μ_B`.
      -- So `B (V i) = μ_B (V i)`.
      let p := (Fintype.equivOfCardEq (LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis.proof_1 d 𝕜 A B hA hB (Matrix.commute_euclideanLin hAB))).symm i
      let μ_B := (LinearMap.IsSymmetric.directSum_isInternal_of_commute' (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA) (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB) (commute_euclideanLin hAB)).subordinateOrthonormalBasis_index (hA.orthogonalFamily_eigenspace_inf_eigenspace' hB (commute_euclideanLin hAB)) p
      -- This is getting complicated to reconstruct the eigenvalue.
      -- But we only need to show `E.toMatrix V * (B * V i)` is diagonal.
      -- If `B * V i = lam * V i`, then `E.toMatrix V * (lam * V i) = lam * e_i`.
      -- Which matches the diagonal form.
      -- We just need `∃ lam, B *ᵥ V i = lam • V i`.
      have h_eig : ∃ lam, B *ᵥ V i = lam • V i := by
        have := (LinearMap.IsSymmetric.directSum_isInternal_of_commute' (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA) (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB) (commute_euclideanLin hAB)).subordinateOrthonormalBasis_spec (hA.orthogonalFamily_eigenspace_inf_eigenspace' hB (commute_euclideanLin hAB)) p
        rw [DirectSum.IsInternal.subordinateOrthonormalBasis_apply] at this
        -- `this` says `V i` is in `eigenspace ...`.
        simp only [LinearMap.mem_eigenspace_iff] at this
        use μ_B.1.2
        -- Convert from LinearMap to Matrix
        rw [← Matrix.toEuclideanLin_apply_toEuclideanLin, commute_iff_eq.mp (commute_euclideanLin hAB)] at this
        -- Wait, `this` is `B.toEuclideanLin (V i) = ...`.
        -- `V` is orthonormal basis of `EuclideanSpace`.
        rw [← Matrix.toEuclideanLin_apply]
        convert this.2
        simp
      obtain ⟨lam, hlam⟩ := h_eig
      rw [hlam]
    rw [h_eig_B, Matrix.mulVec_smul]
    have h2 : E.toMatrix V.toBasis *ᵥ (V i) = Pi.single i 1 := by
      conv_lhs => rw [← Basis.repr_eq_self E (V i)]
      rw [Basis.toMatrix_mulVec_repr, Basis.repr_self, Finsupp.single_eq_pi_single]
      rfl
    rw [h2]
    simp only [EuclideanSpace.single, EuclideanSpace.toLp_single, PiLp.smul_apply, Pi.smul_apply,
      Pi.single_apply, smul_eq_mul, mul_ite, mul_one, mul_zero]
